svg.text: 31. Latency Optimization
Latency = Time from request sent → response received
Goal: Reduce every component of this journey — network, compute, data access, cold starts
✅ Tip 1: Use DAX (In-Memory Cache — microsecond latency)
• DynamoDB default: ~1–10ms | DAX cache hit: <microseconds (up to 10x faster)
• App → DAX cluster endpoint (instead of DynamoDB endpoint directly)
• Best for: Read-heavy workloads, hot key mitigation, gaming leaderboards, real-time feeds
✅ Tip 2: Always Query — Never Scan at Scale
• Scan: Reads EVERY item in table → O(n) cost and time — gets worse as table grows
• Query: Uses index → reads ONLY your partition → O(log n) in B-tree → constant fast
• Fix: Design GSI for every access pattern → Query on GSI instead of Scan on full table
✅ Tip 3: Use ProjectionExpression (Fetch Only What You Need)
• Without: Fetching 400KB item when you need only 3 fields → wastes bandwidth + RCU
• With: ProjectionExpression: "name, email, status" → smaller response → lower latency
• Savings: 400KB item → 2KB payload = 200x less data transferred
✅ Tip 4: SDK Connection Reuse + Singleton Client
• Each new TLS handshake adds 50–200ms — avoid in hot paths
• Use https.Agent({ keepAlive: true }) → persistent HTTP connection pool
• Singleton DynamoDBClient → reused across Lambda invocations (no re-init overhead)
✅ Tip 5: Lambda Provisioned Concurrency (Eliminate Cold Starts)
• Cold Start: Request → no warm Lambda → init container → load code → run → +500ms–2s delay
• Provisioned Concurrency: Keep N Lambda instances pre-initialized → instant execution, 0 cold start
• Note: Costs money (pay for idle warm instances) — use for latency-sensitive production APIs only
Latency Optimization Stack:
DAX (μs cache) → Query/GSI (avoid scan) → ProjectionExpression (less data) → Connection reuse → Provisioned concurrency
Apply all layers together for sub-millisecond p99 latency at scale
